title: populus × canescens
intro: Populus × canescens, commonly known as black cottonwood or canyon cottonwood, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the Salicaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Populus × canescens include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory symptoms, while its bioactive constituents, including salicin and flavonoids, contribute to its medicinal properties. The bioactive constituents in Populus × canescens are responsible for its potential therapeutic applications, and further research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
benefits: Populus × canescens has many benefits, such as its ability to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, while also showing anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. This plant has also been known to have antioxidant properties, which can protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Furthermore, the extracts from Populus × canescens have been used to treat a variety of other health issues, including anxiety and depression, and have even been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing. By incorporating this plant into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
actions: Populus × canescens has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. Its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain, making it a potential treatment for arthritis and other inflammatory diseases, allowing individuals to maintain their mobility and engage in daily activities with greater ease. The antimicrobial properties of Populus × canescens can also aid in wound healing, reduce the risk of infections, and promote oral health, enabling individuals to enjoy a healthy smile and a reduced risk of oral infections. Furthermore, its antioxidant properties can protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, potentially reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease.
intro_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Populus × canescens, specifically the cross between Populus tacamahaca and Populus trichocarpa, exhibits the strongest antibacterial activity, which is supposedly connected with the presence of flavonoids and hydroxycinnamic acids monoesters.
constituents: Populus × canescens has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain in individuals with arthritis. The plant also contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps alleviate headaches and fever, improving daily lives by allowing people to function normally. In addition, Populus × canescens contains phenolic acids, such as ferulic and sinapic acid, which have antioxidant properties, protecting against cell damage and promoting overall health. The plant's rich composition of medicinal constituents also includes phytosterols, like beta-sitosterol, which help lower cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and improving heart health.
parts: Populus × canescens has many medicinal parts, such as the bark, leaves, and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The bark of Populus × canescens contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The roots of Populus × canescens contain phenolic acids and alkaloids, which have been used to treat various conditions, including fever and digestive issues.
preparations: Populus × canescens has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are derived from various parts of the plant, including its bark, leaves, and roots. A tea made from the bark of Populus × canescens is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and irritable bowel syndrome, while a tincture of the roots is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for relieving pain and reducing swelling. The leaves of the plant can be made into an infusion, which is said to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate anxiety and insomnia. Additionally, a salve made from the leaves and bark of Populus × canescens is used to treat wounds and skin irritations, such as eczema and acne.
side_effects: Populus × canescens can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. Ingesting large quantities of the plant's bark or leaves can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while an allergic response may cause symptoms like hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. The plant's salicylic acid content can also cause stomach ulcers and bleeding in some individuals, particularly when taken in excess. Additionally, populus × canescens may interact with blood thinners, such as warfarin, and other medications that affect blood clotting.
